title: Form a Precipitate
description: Capture precipitation evidence, classify results against clear acceptance bounds, and recover safely when outcomes drift.

dialogue.text: Set up two clear salt solutions, PPE, and labeled beakers. We are not done when cloudiness appears—we need interpretable evidence.
Combine solutions slowly while stirring. Capture both precipitate appearance and a pH reading from the supernatant as your evidence pair.

options.text: Measure supernatant pH and log the reading.
I captured a visible precipitate plus a stable reading.
Mixture stayed clear or clumped unpredictably—run troubleshooting.

dialogue.text: Interpretation gate: pass only if a fine, persistent precipitate forms and pH lands in the 6.0–8.0 verification window. Out-of-range readings or unstable solids must be corrected before completion.

options.text: Transfer solids and supernatant to a labeled vial for traceable disposal.
Pass: precipitate is stable and pH is 6.0–8.0.
Fail: artifact quality or pH window missed—correct and retest.

dialogue.text: Corrective branch: stop additions, isolate suspect reagent, and remix with fresh measured volumes. If splashes occurred, decontaminate first. If two runs fail, log and pause for supervisor review.

options.text: Corrective actions complete—run another precipitation trial.
Paused safely after logging repeated failed runs.

dialogue.text: Good chemistry discipline: you captured evidence, interpreted it against explicit bounds, and used a controlled recovery loop when results were unreliable.
